seoTitle: Plympton vs Point Boston — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Plympton and Point Boston. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Plympton vs Point Boston: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Plympton and Point Boston.
heading: Plympton vs Point Boston — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Plympton, South Australia and Point Boston, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Point Boston has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Plympton (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Plympton or Point Boston safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Plympton has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Point Boston scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Plympton scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. Point Boston scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Plympton or Point Boston?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Point Boston has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
